threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and look puzzled when it takes longer than five minutes. Give it time, mate, that's just how it goes.
Rode the 59 past Coldharbour Lane; saw a couple arguing over who gets the last slice of pizza. Bet they didn’t notice the bus driver smirking.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the U-Bahn stopped at Hermannplatz and everyone just kept talking, like the train's presence was as normal as a dog barking.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a döner shop open at 4 AM, serving fresh meat to those stumbling home from a techno club. It's a rite of passage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you don’t flinch at the BART delays but lose it over a dog on the Muni tracks—priorities, right?

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: You know it’s bad when the dog on the tracks is the highlight of the delays. Muni's got us all playing the waiting game again.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the best jamón is at La Boqueria – but locals know the real gems are the tiny shops in Gràcia that sell it by the slice.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they order their vermut—too much ice, no idea about the olive garnish. It's a small detail, but it sticks out.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 10-minute walk is too far and ask locals for directions to the nearest tube station.
Last night on the 333, a bloke argued passionately with the driver about the best Jamaican jerk. Didn't stop until we hit Streatham. Classic Brixton!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using a cardboard box as a makeshift sled down the hill in Hasenheide park. Pure joy in the chaos of the city.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that opens at 5 AM for the after-party crowd, serving sauce-saturated dürüm while locals stumble in, still buzzing.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they don’t flinch at a BART delay but lose their minds over a dog on the tracks at Civic Center.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 at Civic Center just as a guy with a full saxophone ensemble set up to busk. Muni delays turned into a spontaneous mini-concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the plazas and bars, but the real heartbeat is in the hidden alleys, where the old residents still hang laundry out to dry.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their hesitation at the tram stops; locals just glide on without checking the signs. It's a giveaway.
